snapshot.stmt: Suppose \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(2u\le m^\gamma+1\). Then \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\). For every \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), there exists a law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) for which \(\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\). Moreover, among strictly positive tables for \((U,A,Z)\), the rank-deficient tables are contained in the zero set of a nonzero maximal-minor numerator polynomial. Hence full row rank holds on a nonempty relative Zariski-open and Euclidean-dense subset of the positive table simplex, and every table in this subset admits a completion in \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\).

defs.def:pairing-map: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}:\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\to\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)^\ast\), with \([\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}g](s)=\mathbb E_P[s(A,Z)g(U,A,C_\alpha)]\).
defs.def:pairing-matrix: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=[\mathbb E_P\{s_j(A,Z)g_i(U,A,C_\alpha)\}]_{i,j}\), where \((g_i)_i\) is a basis of \(\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\) and \((s_j)_j\) is a basis of \(\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)\).
defs.def:compatible-minor-handle: For fixed \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), parameterize strictly positive finite conditional tables for \(U,A,Z\), couple \(Y(0),Y(1),W\) conditionally on \(U,C_\alpha\), impose the member properties defining \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\), and seek a \(Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)-minor of \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\) whose numerator polynomial is not identically zero on that compatible completion parameter space.

snapshot.stmt: Let \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\). First, if \(2u\le m^\gamma+1\), then \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), and there exists a law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) such that \(\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\). Second, at the endpoint \(\gamma=K\), compatible full-row-rank attainment occurs if and only if \(u\le m^K\), equivalently if and only if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(K)\ge0\). Thus the endpoint conclusion strictly extends the preceding sufficient condition when \((m^K+1)/2<u\le m^K\). In each of these attainment regimes, the full-row-rank tables form a nonempty relative Zariski-open and Euclidean-dense subset of the strictly positive \((U,A,Z)\)-table simplex, and every table in that subset admits a completion in \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\). No conclusion is asserted here for the remaining cases \(\gamma<K\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), and \(2u>m^\gamma+1\).

defs.def:fortified-space:
\(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)=\{d\in L_P^2(A,Z):\mathbb E_P[d(A,Z)\mid Z_{-
u}]=0	ext{ for every }
u\subseteq[K]	ext{ with }|
u|=\gamma\}\)
defs.def:pairing-map: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}:\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\to\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)^\ast\), with \([\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}g](s)=\mathbb E_P[s(A,Z)g(U,A,C_\alpha)]\).
defs.def:pairing-matrix: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=[\mathbb E_P\{s_j(A,Z)g_i(U,A,C_\alpha)\}]_{i,j}\), where \((g_i)_i\) is a basis of \(\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\) and \((s_j)_j\) is a basis of \(\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)\).
defs.def:compatible-minor-handle: For fixed \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), parameterize strictly positive finite conditional tables for \(U,A,Z\), couple \(Y(0),Y(1),W\) conditionally on \(U,C_\alpha\), impose the member properties defining \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\), and seek a \(Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)-minor of \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\) whose numerator polynomial is not identically zero on that compatible completion parameter space.

snapshot.stmt: For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\) such that \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), there exists a law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) for which \[\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma).\] Moreover, after positive conditional tables are parameterized as in Definition \(\mathrm{def:compatible-minor-handle}\) and the positive denominators are cleared, the compatible parameters at which row rank fails form a proper real algebraic subset. Consequently row-rank attainment holds on a nonempty Zariski-open subset, and hence on a Euclidean-dense full-Lebesgue-measure subset, of the positive compatible factor-table parameter space.

defs.def:coordinate-kernels: \(\mathbb R^{\{0,1\}}=\langle\mathbf1_A\rangle\oplus E_A^0\), where \(E_A^0=\ker(x\mapsto\sum_{a=0}^1x_a)\), and \(\mathbb R^m=\langle\mathbf1_i\rangle\oplus E_i^0\), where \(E_i^0=\ker(x\mapsto\sum_{z_i=1}^m x_{z_i})\). Thus \(\dim E_A^0=1\) and \(\dim E_i^0=m-1\).
defs.def:fortified-space:
\(\mathcal H_\gamma(P)=\{d\in L_P^2(A,Z):\mathbb E_P[d(A,Z)\mid Z_{-
u}]=0	ext{ for every }
u\subseteq[K]	ext{ with }|
u|=\gamma\}\)
defs.def:pairing-map: \(\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}:\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\to\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)^\ast\), with \([\mathsf T_{P,\alpha}g](s)=\mathbb E_P[s(A,Z)g(U,A,C_\alpha)]\).
defs.def:pairing-matrix: \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=[\mathbb E_P\{s_j(A,Z)g_i(U,A,C_\alpha)\}]_{i,j}\), where \((g_i)_i\) is a basis of \(\mathcal G_\alpha(P)\) and \((s_j)_j\) is a basis of \(\mathcal S_{\gamma,\alpha}(P)\).
defs.def:compatible-minor-handle: For fixed \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), parameterize strictly positive finite conditional tables for \(U,A,Z\), couple \(Y(0),Y(1),W\) conditionally on \(U,C_\alpha\), impose the member properties defining \(\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\), and seek a \(Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\)-minor of \(M_\gamma(P,\alpha)\) whose numerator polynomial is not identically zero on that compatible completion parameter space. When \(\gamma=K\), also record the two \(m^K\)-by-\(u\) conditional-probability matrices \(\Pi_a=[P(U=v\mid A=a,Z=z)]_{z,v}\), one for each \(a\in\{0,1\}\), as the endpoint block variables. For \(\gamma<K\), retain the maximal-minor search on the full fortified supply matrix.

snapshot.stmt: For fixed \(K,m,u\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma+1)>\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) for every \(\gamma\in\{1,\ldots,K-1\}\). Hence \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is the unique nonnegative-slack boundary, and every selected valid-subset component whose cardinality is strictly below \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) has the universal rank barrier in Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:universal-rank-barrier}\) at each of its laws satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\).

For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), there exists a law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) and \[\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\] if and only if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\). When \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), after the positive conditional tables are parameterized as in Definition \(\mathrm{def:compatible-minor-handle}\) and positive denominators are cleared, the compatible parameters at which row rank fails form a proper real algebraic subset. Thus row-rank attainment holds on a nonempty relative Zariski-open, Euclidean-dense, full-Lebesgue-measure subset of the positive compatible factor-table parameter space. When \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)<0\), the row-rank-attaining locus is empty.

For \(\gamma<K\), retain the maximal-minor search on the full fortified supply matrix.

snapshot.stmt: For fixed \(K,m,u\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma+1)>\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) for every \(\gamma\in\{1,\ldots,K-1\}\). Hence \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is the unique nonnegative-slack boundary, and every selected valid-subset component whose cardinality is strictly below \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) has the universal rank barrier in Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:universal-rank-barrier}\) at each of its laws satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\).

For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), \(u\ge1\), \(\gamma\in[K]\), and \(\alpha\in\mathcal A_\gamma\), there exists a law \(P\in\mathcal P_{\gamma,\alpha}\) satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\) and \[\operatorname{rank}M_\gamma(P,\alpha)=Q_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\] if and only if \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\). When \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\ge0\), after the positive conditional tables are parameterized as in Definition \(\mathrm{def:compatible-minor-handle}\) and positive denominators are cleared, the compatible parameters at which row rank fails form a proper real algebraic subset. Thus row-rank attainment holds on a nonempty relative Zariski-open, Euclidean-dense, full-Lebesgue-measure subset of the positive compatible factor-table parameter space. When \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)<0\), the row-rank-attaining locus is empty.

snapshot.stmt: For fixed \(K,m,u\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma+1)>\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) for every \(\gamma\in\{1,\ldots,K-1\}\). Hence \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is the unique nonnegative-slack boundary, and every selected valid-subset component whose cardinality is strictly below \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) has the universal rank barrier in Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:universal-rank-barrier}\) at each of its laws satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\).

For every \(K\ge2\), \(m\ge2\), and \(u\ge1\), \[\Delta_{K,m,u}(K)=2(m^K-u).\] Consequently, \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)<+\infty\) if and only if \(u\le m^K\). If \(u=m^K\), then \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)=K\).

For fixed \(K,m,u\), \(\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma+1)>\Delta_{K,m,u}(\gamma)\) for every \(\gamma\in\{1,\ldots,K-1\}\). Hence \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\), when finite, is the unique nonnegative-slack boundary, and every selected valid-subset component whose cardinality is strictly below \(\gamma_{\mathrm{dim}}(K,m,u)\) has the universal rank barrier in Theorem \(\mathrm{thm:universal-rank-barrier}\) at each of its laws satisfying Assumption \(\mathrm{ass:full-cell-support}\).
